frameworks.name: deterministic V1-to-choice linkage
frameworks.one_line: view that V1 neural activity directly determines trial-by-trial perceptual choices

frameworks.name: population decoding of human V1
frameworks.one_line: reading stimulus and choice signals from human V1 ensemble activity

frameworks.name: choice probability / choice-related activity
frameworks.one_line: framework for relating sensory neuron activity to perceptual decisions

core_question: Do stimulus-related and choice-related signals in human V1 share the same neural ensemble and timing during a difficult perceptual discrimination?
key_assumptions: V1 distributes sensory representations to higher visual areas
deterministic linkage predicts co-localized stimulus and choice signals in V1
population-level fMRI activity can index trial-by-trial sensory and choice variability
manipulations: difficult visual discrimination task with trial-by-trial choice variability
comparison of stimulus-driven versus choice-correlated V1 population responses
key_findings: choice-related activity was present in human V1 during the discrimination task
stimulus-related activity was carried by a distinct V1 neural ensemble
choice-related activity was advanced in time relative to stimulus-related activity
interpretations: V1 choice signals do not arise from the same neurons that encode the task-relevant stimulus difference
findings contradict a strictly deterministic V1-to-choice account
